steps.title: Find the rate of change
steps.content:
The function passes through the point with input $4$ and output $190$, and the point with input $10$ and output $670$. The slope is the change in profit divided by the change in items sold.
$$m=\frac{670-190}{10-4}=\frac{480}{6}=80$$

steps.title: Find the starting value
steps.content:
Use $f(x)=80x+b$ and substitute the point where $x=4$ and $f(x)=190$.
$$190=80(4)+b$$
$$190=320+b$$
$$b=-130$$
So the equation is $f(x)=80x-130$.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content:
The line through the two given points is $y=80x-130$. It checks both profits:
$$80(4)-130=190$$
$$80(10)-130=670$$
The answer is D.
